metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'compare git log for the paths' in AC6 with a concrete git ancestry command, e.g. `git merge-base --is-ancestor $(git log -1 --format=%H -- CONTRIBUTING.md) $(git log -1 --format=%H -- infra/gerrit/project.config) && git merge-base --is-ancestor $(git log -1 --format=%H -- infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e.sh) $(git log -1 --format=%H -- infra/gerrit/project.config)` (exits 0 only if project.config commit is a descendant of the named paths).
findings.finding: AC6's proving command is 'compare git log for the paths' — no concrete shell command is given, making it impossible to deterministically verify the migration-order constraint in-session.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC6 (Migration order provable from history)
findings.suggested_fix: prove: for each of the three e2e scripts and CONTRIBUTING.md, run `git merge-base --is-ancestor $(git log -1 --format=%H -- <path>) $(git log -1 --format=%H -- infra/gerrit/project.config)`; all four commands must exit 0.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC3's 'one e2e suite run passes post-flip' to state the intended scope — if all three suites must pass, write 'all three e2e suites pass post-flip'; if only one is feasible in-session, name it and document why the others are excluded.
findings.finding: AC3 states 'one e2e suite run passes post-flip' but there are three distinct e2e scripts; it is ambiguous whether 'one' means any single suite suffices or all three must pass.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 (e2e scripts sign their commits)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'one e2e suite run passes post-flip' with 'all three e2e suites (reviewbot-e2e.sh, feature-branch-e2e.sh, feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh) pass post-flip, proving each script's -s fix is exercised'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the automation inventory to acknowledge that `infra/scripts/bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh` and `.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml` also contain `git commit` invocations, and explicitly note why each does NOT need `-s` (they push to `refs/meta/config` and a GitHub branch respectively, not to Gerrit's `refs/for/*` where `requireSignedOffBy` is enforced).
findings.finding: The plan claims 'the three e2e scripts above are the known set' of commit-creating automation that pushes to refs/for/*. However, the codebase contains additional 'git commit' invocations the plan's own inventory grep ('rg -l "git commit" infra/ scripts/ .github/ Makefile') would surface at execution time: (a) infra/scripts/bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh line 68 commits and pushes to a Gerrit ref (refs/meta/config, not refs/for/*); (b) .github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml line 242 commits and pushes to a GitHub branch (not Gerrit). Neither pushes to refs/for/* so requireSignedOffBy doesn't apply to them, but the plan's pre-assertion 'three e2e scripts are the known set' is demonstrably incorrect — the grep would find more.
findings.location: What section — 'Automation inventory (decision rule)': 'the three e2e scripts above are the known set; anything newly found is updated the same way (add -s) or ticketed via discovered_from'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix AC3's grep target for `feature-branch-e2e.sh`: since all `git commit` calls are in the sourced `feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh`, redirect the AC grep to that file (or add a note that `feature-branch-e2e.sh` itself has no commits and the check applies to `feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh` instead).
findings.finding: The AC for `feature-branch-e2e.sh` checks for `-s` in that file directly, but `feature-branch-e2e.sh` contains zero `git commit` invocations — all commits are made in the sourced `feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh`. The AC grep (`grep -q "commit .*-s\b\|commit -s" infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e.sh`) will always return no match regardless of whether the fix is applied correctly.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 / e2e scripts sign their commits; infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e.sh
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC grep for `feature-branch-e2e.sh` with: `grep -q "commit .*-s\b\|commit -s" infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh` — that is where all the `git commit` calls actually live.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten AC2's evidentiary standard: require the literal server rejection message (e.g. captured via `git push 2>&1 | tee /tmp/reject.txt`) to appear verbatim in the documented artifact, and name a single canonical location (e.g. `docs/dco-verification.md`) rather than offering a disjunctive 'or'.
findings.finding: AC2's evidentiary standard is disjunctive ('server message captured in the commit message OR a docs/ note'), leaving the executor free to choose the weaker option; a docs/ note created manually does not constitute reproducible proof that the server actually rejected the push.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the rationale in the plan's 'Declarative config path' paragraph: `setup-project.sh` is idempotent (skips the push when `project.config` is already up to date), not 'overwrites on every run' — the correct rationale is that a manual push outside `setup-project.sh` would be overwritten on the next script execution when any other change triggers a run.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'setup-project.sh overwrites project.config on every run' as its rationale for why a manual push would be silently reverted. The actual code (lines 135-138 of setup-project.sh) shows it short-circuits with 'already up to date (no-op)' when the staged diff is clean — it does NOT overwrite on every run. While this inaccuracy is benign for the specific change (adding a new line will produce a diff), the stated rationale is factually wrong and could mislead anyone reasoning about the script's behavior for future changes.
findings.location: What section — 'Declarative config path' paragraph: 'setup-project.sh overwrites project.config on every run; a one-off manual push would be silently reverted'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the rationale to: 'Rationale: `setup-project.sh` is the single source of truth for `refs/meta/config`; a one-off manual push would be silently overwritten whenever any other config change triggers a `setup-project.sh` run.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `-E` to the AC3 grep proving command and escape accordingly: `grep -qE 'commit[[:space:]].*-s\b|commit[[:space:]]-s' infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e.sh` (and same for the other two files), ensuring portability across GNU and BSD grep.
findings.finding: AC3's grep pattern uses BRE alternation and word-boundary (`\|` and `\b`) without the `-E` flag; on non-GNU grep (e.g. macOS BSD grep) these are not recognized, making the proving command silently fail or produce false negatives.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 (e2e scripts sign their commits)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix AC7's grep to use `-E` for portable alternation: `grep -qE 'Sign your work|DCO' docs/your-first-change.md`.
findings.finding: AC7's grep pattern uses shell-level `\|` inside a single-quoted string — `grep -q "Sign your work\|DCO"` — which passes a literal backslash-pipe to BRE grep; on BSD grep this is two separate characters (matches 'Sign your work' or '|DCO' unexpectedly) rather than alternation, making the command unreliable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC7 (Tutorial retarget done after dependency)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten the automation-inventory decision rule to clarify that only `git commit` invocations whose output is subsequently pushed to a Gerrit `refs/for/*` ref require `-s`; explicitly exclude `infra/scripts/bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh` (NoteDb admin push) and `.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml` (GitHub tickets branch) from the update list.
findings.finding: The plan's automation-inventory decision rule ('anything newly found is updated the same way — add -s') does not distinguish commits that feed into refs/for/* (subject to requireSignedOffBy) from commits that push to other transports. Two out-of-scope callers surfaced by the plan's own scan command — infra/scripts/bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh (pushes to Gerrit NoteDb admin refs, not refs/for/*) and .github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml (pushes to the GitHub tickets branch) — both contain git commit invocations and would be found at execution time, but adding -s to them is unnecessary (they are never rejected by requireSignedOffBy). The plan names neither file, and the blanket rule does not tell the implementor to skip them.
findings.location: What section — 'Automation inventory (decision rule)' paragraph; also AC bullet for e2e scripts
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-sentence qualifier: 'Only commits subsequently pushed to `refs/for/*` are subject to `requireSignedOffBy`; commits to NoteDb admin refs (bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh) and GitHub non-Gerrit branches (reconcile-bridge.yml) are excluded.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Refine the automation inventory decision rule to scope it: the criterion for 'add `-s`' should be 'git commit invocations in scripts that push to Gerrit `refs/for/*`', not all `git commit` occurrences under `infra/ scripts/ .github/`; explicitly exempt `setup-project.sh` (`refs/meta/config`), `probe-rebar.sh` (local-only), and `reconcile-bridge.yml` (GitHub `tickets` branch).
findings.finding: The automation inventory decision rule (`rg -l 'git commit' infra/ scripts/ .github/ Makefile`) will surface at least four additional files beyond the three named e2e scripts — `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh`, `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh`, `scripts/probe-rebar.sh`, and `.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml` — and the plan's fallback ('anything newly found is updated the same way: add `-s`') does not distinguish commits that push to Gerrit `refs/for/*` (where `requireSignedOffBy` applies) from those that push to `refs/meta/config`, GitHub `tickets` branch, or a local probe repo. Indiscriminate `-s` addition to non-Gerrit commits could produce spurious trailers, and the `setup-project.sh` commit (pushing to `refs/meta/config`) may not accept a `Signed-off-by` trailer in the standard DCO sense.
findings.location: What section — 'Automation inventory (decision rule): enumerate … anything newly found is updated the same way (add `-s`) or ticketed'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit triage criterion to the automation inventory decision rule: specify that only `git commit` invocations whose output is subsequently pushed to a Gerrit `refs/for/*` ref require `-s`; list the already-confirmed exempt paths (setup-project.sh → refs/meta/config, service-user.sh → refs/meta/config, bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh → refs/users/*, reconcile-bridge.yml → GitHub) so the executor can apply the rule without ambiguity.
findings.finding: The automation inventory decision rule ('add -s or ticket it') lacks a triage criterion distinguishing which discovered git-commit invocations actually require a Signed-off-by trailer. The plan's own prescribed sweep (`rg -l 'git commit' infra/ scripts/ .github/ Makefile`) yields commits in scripts/probe-rebar.sh, infra/scripts/bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh, infra/gerrit/service-user.sh, and .github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml — none of these push to refs/for/* (the path where requireSignedOffBy is enforced); applying -s to them is unnecessary but the plan gives no protocol to make that determination.
findings.suggested_fix: Amend the automation inventory bullet to read: 'Decision rule: add `-s` ONLY to commits that are pushed (directly or via `git push … HEAD:refs/for/*`) to a Gerrit review ref — `requireSignedOffBy` is enforced at that receive path. Commits pushed to `refs/meta/config`, `refs/users/*`, or to GitHub are NOT subject to this enforcement and do NOT need `-s`. Known-exempt: setup-project.sh, service-user.sh, bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh, reconcile-bridge.yml.'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Sharpen the automation inventory decision rule to filter specifically for commits pushed to Gerrit `refs/for/*` (not all `git commit` occurrences); note that `scripts/probe-rebar.sh` and `.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml` commit to non-Gerrit destinations and are out of scope for `-s` fixes.
findings.finding: The automation inventory decision rule ('add -s or ticket via discovered_from') is under-specified: the `rg -l "git commit" infra/ scripts/ .github/ Makefile` sweep will surface commits that are NOT pushed to Gerrit `refs/for/*` — specifically `scripts/probe-rebar.sh` (commits to a temp local repo, never Gerrit-bound) and `.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml` (commits to a `.tickets-tracker` submodule pushed to GitHub `origin/tickets`, not Gerrit). Applying `-s` to those is harmless but wasteful; more importantly, an implementer applying the rule mechanically may waste time or introduce confusion. The correct filter criterion is: 'is this commit eventually pushed to a Gerrit `refs/for/*` ref?' — only that subset is subject to `requireSignedOffBy`.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the decision rule with: 'enumerate automation that both (a) creates commits and (b) pushes them to a Gerrit `refs/for/*` ref; only that subset needs `-s`. Commits pushed to `refs/meta/config` (admin path), to GitHub directly, or to local temp repos are not subject to `requireSignedOffBy`.' Then note explicitly that `scripts/probe-rebar.sh` and `.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml` are out of scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact placement of the agent trailer guidance within CLAUDE.md — name the target section/step (e.g., 'insert after step 2 of the Git workflow section' or 'add a bullet to the existing commit step') so the instruction is co-located with the commit workflow an agent reads when making a commit.
findings.finding: The plan says the agent trailer will be 'Documented in CLAUDE.md's commit guidance verbatim' but does not specify the placement within CLAUDE.md. CLAUDE.md has a 'Git workflow (code changes)' section with step-by-step commit instructions; if the trailer line is added to a different section (e.g., appended at EOF or under a new heading), an agent following the file may not encounter it at the point-of-use during a commit.
findings.location: What section — 'Agent commits (RebarBotNava / joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com committer)' bullet; AC5

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Address the `git commit --amend -m "...Change-Id: ..."` pattern in `feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh` line 174 explicitly: either embed `Signed-off-by:` manually in the inline `-m` message (before the `Change-Id:` line) rather than relying on `-s`, or restructure the amend to not use an explicit `-m` so `-s` can safely append the trailer.
findings.finding: `feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh` line 174 contains a bare `git commit -q --amend -m "..."` (the re-merge amend for scenario 4) that the plan's `-s` fix must address, but this invocation reconstructs the full commit message inline (including the Change-Id), making a naive append of `-s` incorrect — `git commit --amend -s` with an explicit `-m` that doesn't already include the trailer would append a duplicate or misplaced `Signed-off-by` after the `Change-Id`, violating DCO trailer ordering conventions.
findings.location: What section — 'e2e test scripts … each script adds `-s` to its `git commit` invocations'; infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh line 174

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies the shepherded-patch two-trailer rule is present in CONTRIBUTING.md (e.g., `grep -qi "shepherd\|two.*sign\|sign.*twice" CONTRIBUTING.md` or an equivalent prose check).
findings.finding: The shepherded-patch two-trailer rule (contributor's own sign-off required; shepherd adds theirs on amend) is described as a deliverable in the 'What' section but has no covering acceptance criterion — it is an orphaned deliverable.
findings.location: What section (shepherded-patch two-trailer rule); Acceptance Criteria (no covering AC)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: '- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md shepherded-patch rule present — prove: `grep -qi "shepherd" CONTRIBUTING.md` (or a more specific string matching the exact wording chosen).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a grep check in AC #4 for the real-name requirement text (e.g., `grep -qi "real.name\|legal name" CONTRIBUTING.md`).
findings.finding: The 'real-name requirement' is described as a required element of the CONTRIBUTING.md DCO section ('real-name requirement' is listed alongside `git commit -s`, the link to developercertificate.org, etc.) but no AC verifies its presence.
findings.location: What section ('real-name requirement'); Acceptance Criteria AC #4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a citation for the 'kernel/DCO practice accepts plus-addressed emails' claim — either link a kernel list post, a DCO FAQ, or reframe the rationale as 'RFC 5321-valid address; Gerrit enforces trailer presence only, not address format' to make it grounded and accurate.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'kernel practice accepts plus-addresses' (for the agent sign-off trailer `joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com`) as established fact satisfying DCO 1.1, but provides no citation. The Linux kernel's submitting-patches documentation does not explicitly address plus-addressed emails, and DCO 1.1 itself says nothing about email address formats. While Gerrit only checks that a `Signed-off-by:` trailer is present (not that it matches any RFC), the claim that plus-addressing is accepted practice under the DCO is an unresolved capability assertion.
findings.location: What section — 'Agent commits' bullet: 'kernel practice accepts plus-addresses; the certifier is the human, satisfying DCO 1.1'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'kernel practice accepts plus-addresses' with: 'RFC 5321 treats `user+tag@domain` as a valid mailbox routing to the base address; Gerrit's `requireSignedOffBy` enforces trailer presence only (not address format); the certifier is the human.' This is accurate without needing an external citation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a short ADR or `docs/decisions/` entry (or expand the CONTRIBUTING.md DCO section beyond the one-liner) capturing the key rationale: DCO 1.1 human-certifier interpretation for agent commits, the plus-address precedent, why enforcement is in `project.config` not `gerrit.config`, and the OpenStack/Qt evidence for DCO-over-CLA — so future maintainers have in-repo provenance for these governance choices.
findings.finding: The DCO adoption rationale, agent sign-off policy reasoning (DCO 1.1 human-certifier interpretation, kernel plus-address precedent, OpenStack precedent), and the decision to use per-project Gerrit config rather than a site-level setting are not captured in any durable in-repo ADR or design doc — only a one-line CLA decision record ('rebar uses the DCO, not a CLA') lands in CONTRIBUTING.md. Future maintainers who need to revisit the agent trailer format, challenge the plus-address interpretation, or understand why enforcement lives in project.config rather than gerrit.config will find no in-repo rationale.
findings.location: What section — 'CLA decision record' bullet; Scope section; AC item 5 (CLAUDE.md)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `docs/decisions/dco-adoption.md` (or `docs/adr/NNN-dco.md`) with: (1) context — no prior IP mechanism; (2) decision — DCO via Gerrit `requireSignedOffBy`; (3) agent policy — human certifier per DCO 1.1(c), plus-address rationale; (4) alternatives considered — CLA rejected (Qt barrier precedent); (5) consequences — new contributors must sign, e2e scripts use `-s`. Reference it from CONTRIBUTING.md's CLA decision record line. This is a one-time ~30-line doc that preserves the rationale durably.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit error handling to the setup-project.sh rollout step: the script should exit non-zero and surface a clear error message on push failure, and the plan should specify that the AC1 live-verification step (unsigned push rejected) is the authoritative confirmation that the flag actually landed in Gerrit — not just the grep on the local file.
findings.finding: The setup-project.sh push to refs/meta/config is a stateful write with no error handling, retry logic, or failure surfacing specified. If the push fails (auth error, network blip, Gerrit unavailable, non-fast-forward conflict), the plan provides no guidance on detecting or recovering from a partial/failed rollout — the config flag may appear to have landed when it hasn't (or vice versa). Similarly, the GitHub web-signoff API toggle has no error handling described.
findings.location: What section — 'Declarative config path' / 'Flip GitHub's require sign-off' / Acceptance Criteria AC1
findings.suggested_fix: In the setup-project.sh rollout description, add: 'The script must exit non-zero on push failure; the operator must observe `remote: error` or a rejection to confirm the flag is absent, and confirm via the live-verification AC (AC2) — not only the local grep — that enforcement is active.' For the GitHub toggle, note that `gh api` exit code should be checked and the AC8 jq check should be run immediately after.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
